summary:
The statement claiming all trauma patients should have a PA-only chest X-ray is false due to lack of support from evidence and deviation from contextualized care priorities such as avoiding delays for unstable patients or prioritizing urgent CT scans when appropriate. In contrast, standard practice recognizes selective use of radiographic exams based on injury mechanisms and stabilization status—especially omitting nonessential studies in unstable individuals—and favors early advanced imaging where clinically indicated. 

Note: This summary focuses exclusively on core clinical decision-making elements related to trauma triage and diagnostic strategy within initial assessment phases without including extraneous details unrelated directly to image selection rationale under emergency conditions.

Final Answer: D

The claim that all trauma patients require a PA-only chest X-ray contradicts best-practice guidelines emphasizing timely, targeted imaging instead of universal application. Instead, management relies on assessing instability first—the need for immediate intervention outweighs routine radiography—which makes exclusive reliance on chest X-rays inappropriate across all scenarios.
